danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to player progression; avoidance or combat required
Instant or progressive damage; threat to health bar

danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy sprite at level end

danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative damage state; imminent death risk if not restored

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible power-ups and potions scattered in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to engage or continue; damage recovery available

safety_signals.visual_form: Skull item sitting on Chuck's shoulders as substitute head
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced offensive capability; temporary invulnerability state
Temporary protective or offensive advantage; safe harbor mechanic
Health restoration point; safe anchor location

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diverse underworld creature designs representing conquest force
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Underworld theming; skeletal island geography fragmented by Max's invasion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Chuck D. Head (protagonist); mad scientist iconography
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle Frank N. Stein as home base; laboratory setting; scientific instrumentation
Island divided into anatomical zones (Abdomainland, Armington); normal topography threatened by underworld incursion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Monster army present in levels; fragmented skeleton-island geography; gothic color palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol and attack; destructive environmental layout; mandatory boss confrontations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Skeleton-shaped landmass split into disconnected pieces; level names reference anatomical regions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must progress through multiple discrete level zones to progress narrative

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Islands reforming; Chuck returns home; transformation into human form narrative reward
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game conclusion; end-state indicates successful faction conquest

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progressive enemy density; boss battles signaling zone completion; health upgrade availability increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player acquires stronger items (skull, potions); difficulty escalation through level sequence

environmental_storytelling.example: Levels named after skeleton parts (Abdomainland, Armington) reinforce that the playable island is literally a skeleton structure threatened by invasion
Dark, spooky aesthetics communicate underworld dominance and monster-filled landscape; contrasts with Dr. Stein's laboratory sanctuary
Chuck D. Head is a headless mummy—living death itself—created to combat underworld threat; his final transformation into human signals world restoration
Skeleton-shaped island split into pieces by Max's invasion visually communicates the severity of the underworld threat and narrative stakes
Each zone ends with a boss encounter, creating visual and mechanical checkpoints that reinforce faction control structure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Side-scrolling movement, face-extension attacks, jump-based platforming with descent control
Throwing boomerang-like skull projectile; enhanced offensive range
Heart count decreases; visual health indicator depletion; vulnerability state persists until potion/flagpole restoration
Patrol levels; initiate combat with player; represent constant threat presence
Boss battle mechanics; commands army; defeated to progress narrative
Issues level objectives (collect items); provides narrative rewards; safe-space presence

notes: Decap Attack uses gothic/macabre visual theming to communicate underworld invasion world-state. Faction conflict is primary narrative driver: Max D. Cap's underworld army vs. Dr. Stein's creation (Chuck) and surface inhabitants. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on skeleton-island metaphor and anatomical level naming to reinforce geographic devastation. Health system (hearts, potions, flagpoles) creates visual danger/safety gradient. Protagonist's headless design and eventual humanization provide character-arc visualization of world restoration. Comic strip adaptation (Sonic the Comic) extended faction characterization but not primary to game mechanics.
